message:
chore(beads): Sync issue tracker with fixture and test infrastructure work

Beads automatic sync reflecting completion of async error path coverage
fixtures and manifest test cases. Updates issue tracker state and metadata
to track progress on feature implementation.

Changes to Issues:
==================

New Issue Created:
- ultimate_bug_scanner-blh: "Initial repo orientation"
  - Type: task
  - Priority: 3 (low)
  - Status: in_progress
  - Assignee: codex
  - Description: Read AGENTS.md, README.md, explore project layout
  - Purpose: Onboarding task for new agent/session

Context:
This issue appears to track initial orientation work, likely created by
another agent instance or earlier in a session that was continued. It's
standard practice in multi-agent workflows to have explicit orientation
issues for transparency.

Existing Issues (no content changes):
- ultimate_bug_scanner-e3j: Async error path coverage (in_progress)
  - Status remains in_progress (awaiting validation run)
  - Next: Run manifest tests to confirm all rules fire
  - Next: Complete Ruby run_async_error_checks() implementation

- ultimate_bug_scanner-d5z: Add manifest coverage (in_progress)
  - Async error manifest cases now complete (14 test cases)
  - Remaining: Expand to other detection categories

- ultimate_bug_scanner-6ig: Resource lifecycle bug (open)
  - Blocks CI integration (#o3l)
  - Needs investigation into zero-files-scanned reports

- ultimate_bug_scanner-o3l: CI integration (open)
  - Blocked by #6ig
  - Manifest runner ready, waiting for fixture bugs to be fixed

Metadata Updates:
=================

- Updated commit reference from 5bd168e → [current commit]
- Updated timestamp to track sync point
- Version remains 0.23.1 (no Beads schema changes)

beads.left.meta.json:
- Updated commit reference from 2aa61a1 → [current commit]
- Updated timestamp to track sync point
- Tracks divergence from base branch

- Priority 3 (low): 1 issue (blh - orientation)

By Type:

Work Completed Since Last Sync (commits 711e92a → HEAD):
=========================================================

This sync captures work from 5 commits:
1. 12d3507: Interpreted language async error fixtures (JS/Python/Ruby)
2. f59787f: Compiled language async error fixtures (C++/Go/Java/Rust)
3. bc45a0c: Manifest test cases (14 entries)
4. 1eb735d: JS module refactoring (rule directory)
5. d7b70d2: TODO documentation updates

Deliverables:
- 14 async error fixtures (buggy + clean for 7 languages)
- 14 manifest test cases (regression + precision tests)
- 1 module refactoring (improved reliability)
- ~700 lines of test code and manifest entries

Impact on Issue ultimate_bug_scanner-e3j:
==========================================

Async error path coverage is now ~90% complete:
- ✅ Scanner implementation: 6/7 languages (Ruby pending)
- ✅ Fixtures: 7/7 languages
- ✅ Manifest tests: 7/7 languages (14 test cases)
- ☐ Validation: Pending manifest runner execution
- ☐ Ruby completion: run_async_error_checks() implementation needed

Next milestone: Run `./test-suite/run_manifest.py --filter async-errors`
and document results in issue notes. If all 14 tests pass, async error
coverage can move to validation/refinement phase.

This sync demonstrates healthy progress on the async error coverage feature,
with test infrastructure complete and only validation + Ruby implementation
remaining before feature can be marked production-ready.

Related:

- Async error coverage tracking: async-error-coverage-todo.md
